title: Nonevaporable getter coating chambers for extreme high vacuum
authors_or_org: Marcy L. Stutzman, Philip A. Adderley, Md Abdullah A. Mamun, Matt Poelker; Journal of Vacuum Science & Technology A (AIP Publishing)
claim_supported: NEG (Ti-Zr-V) coating turns an accelerator beam-pipe chamber into a distributed pump, reducing thermal and beam-stimulated outgassing enough to reach extreme high vacuum without discrete pumps; work by Jefferson Lab's photoinjector group (Stutzman/Adderley/Mamun/Poelker).
locator: Crossref record confirmed this session (DOI 10.1116/1.5010154; JVST A 36(3):031603, 2018; authors Stutzman, Adderley, Mamun, Poelker)
notes: Geography corrected from CH (CERN guess) to US: authors are Jefferson Lab (Newport News, VA), not CERN. This is the same research group as L07-002. An arXiv preprint mirror (1803.07056) is discovery-only; this AIP JVST A record is the peer-reviewed final.

india_origin_audit.notes: All four authors Jefferson Lab, US, confirming the record's own correction from CH to US.

title: A road map to extreme high vacuum
authors_or_org: P. Adderley, G. Myneni; Journal of Physics: Conference Series (IOP Publishing)
claim_supported: Outlines a practical path (helium leak detection, cryosorption, low-cost diffusion barriers, clean assembly) to extend vacuum systems from UHV into the XHV regime, from the Jefferson Lab photoinjector/SRF vacuum group.
locator: Crossref record fetched this session (DOI 10.1088/1742-6596/114/1/012011; JPCS vol 114, 2008; authors Adderley, Myneni). Crossref search on the exact title independently corroborates this author pairing (top-ranked hit).
notes: MAJOR CORRECTION: raw ledger misattributed this paper to P. A. Redhead (National Research Council Canada) and set geography CA. Crossref confirms the actual authors are P. Adderley and G. Myneni (Jefferson Lab, US) -- the same first author as L07-001. Authors and geography corrected accordingly. JPCS is a conference-proceedings journal with lighter editorial review than a full journal, so tier kept at T2 per source standards.

The record's only URL is a ResearchGate upload, which per source standards is discovery-only and cannot be the canonical accepted record for an academic source; direct ResearchGate fetch returned HTTP 403 this session, so the true underlying publisher identity could not be independently confirmed. Rejected as unresolved.
notes: Distinct from the medium-temperature heat-treatment primary study now confirmed as L07-009 (Park/Chung/Liu/Li, JVST A 2008).

BACKLOG FIGURE FLAGGED: raw ledger states order backlog of ¥174.1 billion; this session's search instead surfaced ¥17.41 billion for the same disclosure -- a possible order-of-magnitude (10x) transcription conflict between passes, not resolved against the primary PDF this session.
locator: WebSearch this session corroborated orders/sales figures against ULVAC's own IR press release (ir.ulvac.co.jp, 決算短信 dated 2025-08-13); primary EDINET securities-report PDF not itself line-item-fetched
notes: Given ¥174.1B would represent ~33% of annual sales (a plausible equipment-backlog ratio) versus ¥17.41B representing only ~3% (unusually thin for this business), the original ¥174.1B figure is judged more likely correct, but this is a judgment call, not a confirmed fact -- flagged for resolution against the primary PDF. ULVAC remains one of six vendors said to hold ~62% global semiconductor vacuum-pump revenue share per L07-039 (that specific share figure itself is now flagged as unconfirmed, see L07-039).

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: ULVAC HQ Chigasaki, Kanagawa, Japan; mandatory Japanese securities filing.

title: INFICON Holding AG — Fourth Quarter and Full-Year 2025 Results
authors_or_org: INFICON Holding AG
claim_supported: INFICON (Swiss-listed) reported FY2025 full-year sales of $673.7M (+0.4% YoY), a record; its largest segment, Semi & Vacuum Coating, was 49% of Group sales at $330.0M (down 2.9%, hit by trade/tech disputes); Asia-Pacific sales rose 19.2% to $168.6M on AI-chip demand and a new Malaysia facility; Americas sales fell 12.4% to $156.4M, the weakest region.
locator: eqs-news.com ad-hoc disclosure release fetched directly this session (corrected URL from the raw ledger's 'results will be published' placeholder page to the actual results release); all figures confirmed exactly
notes: CORRECTED URL/LOCATOR: the raw ledger's URL (inficon.com 'to publish its results' announcement) only pre-announced a future publication date and contained none of the actual figures; the actual ad-hoc results release (EQS-News) was located and fetched this session, confirming every figure in the original claim_supported exactly. This is a Swiss mandatory ad-hoc disclosure (SIX Exchange Regulation), equivalent in status to a regulatory filing.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: INFICON HQ Bad Ragaz, Switzerland (SIX-listed, mandatory ad-hoc disclosure). Distribution via EQS Group, HQ Munich, Germany; EQS lists a Kochi, India office among many global offices but is not India-headquartered.

title: SBIR Award: Densified Cryogen System for Maximum Storage and Delivery Performance in Zero-Gravity (Sierra Lobo, Inc.)
authors_or_org: US Department of Defense, Missile Defense Agency (MDA); recipient Sierra Lobo, Inc.
claim_supported: A DOD/Missile Defense Agency SBIR Phase II award ($495,907) funded Sierra Lobo, Inc. (Fremont, Ohio) to develop a densified-cryogen storage system with a lightweight sensor array for cryogenic temperature and vapor/liquid (leak-relevant) detection in zero-gravity.
locator: sbir.gov award record page re-fetched directly this session; title, agency, recipient, amount, phase, and year all confirmed exactly
notes: Re-confirmed this session. Older award (2003); illustrates that government funding for this niche is intermittent rather than a steady recent pipeline — flagged as an uncertainty in the lane brief.

institutions.name: US Department of Defense, Missile Defense Agency
